sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a young adult male, presented with complaints of back pain on the left side, which has been intermittent and sometimes sharp, but not always present.
He reports that his primary care physician (PCP) had him come in for a follow-up visit due to concerns about his symptoms.
The patient denies being more tired than usual and reports that his appetite is unchanged, but mentions that he has been drinking more water and waking up to use the restroom more frequently.

He also reports riding a bike occasionally and has been doing well in school, but mentions that his physics teacher is "cheesy." The patient has a history of recurrent strep throat, with four episodes in the last five months.
His brother smokes, which the patient has tried to convince him to quit.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of recurrent strep throat, with four episodes in the last five months.
He also has a brother who smokes.
Review of systems: The patient denies fever, but mentions that he has been drinking more water and waking up to use the restroom more frequently.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's throat was not erythematous, without swelling, and his tonsils were midline.
There was no lymphadenopathy or cervical adenopathy.
The lungs were clear, and the heart sounds were normal.
The patient did not have tenderness on palpation of the left back or abdomen.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has recurrent strep throat, which may be related to glomerulonephritis, a condition that could lead to chronic kidney disease.
Problem list: Recurrent strep throat; suspected glomerulonephritis; suspected kidney disease.
Plan:
I ordered several labs, including a lipid panel, UA, CMP, CBC, vitamin D, and TSH, to investigate the patient's symptoms and potential underlying conditions.
I referred the patient to an ENT specialist to consider tonsillectomy to help with recurrent strep throat.
I also referred the patient for glomerulonephritis.
Follow-up: The patient is to return in a week for results.
I will discuss the results with the patient over the phone if they are normal, but will require the patient to return if additional conversations are needed.
